items.title: War unlikely to dent Songkran revenue
items.desc: The Tourism Authority of Thailand (TAT) remains confident about its projection of 6% revenue growth tallying 30 billion baht during the Songkran holiday this year, mainly driven by Asian markets.

items.title: Co-pay scheme, emergency projects to get B300bn
items.desc: Roughly 300 billion baht in investment expenditure is expected to be available for transfer to the central budget to support the "Khon La Khrueng" co-payment scheme and urgent government emergency projects.

items.title: Stricter rules for digital platforms
items.desc: After months of anticipation, Thailand has introduced sweeping new trade competition guidelines to govern multi-sided platforms, aiming to reshape the e-commerce landscape.

items.title: Regulator offers 3 power tariff rates
items.desc: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) is preparing to announce a higher power tariff for May to August once it gathers public input on an appropriate rate amid a war in the Middle East.

items.title: Lawmakers urged to cut aide quota
items.desc: The Thai Pakdee Party has urged both MPs and senators to cut the number of personal assistants from eight to three, saying the move could save up to 3 billion baht annually.

items.title: Ministry to limit treatment, expand meds controls
items.desc: The Ministry of Public Health plans to limit certain treatments, expand telemedicine and tighten pharmaceutical management to cope with mounting resource pressures, as the Middle East war threatens global medical supply chains, permanent secretary Dr Somruek Chungsaman said.

items.title: Khon Kaen tourism, meetings take a hit
items.desc: Rising oil prices and the Middle East war are taking a heavy toll on Khon Kaen's tourism and meetings, incentives, conferences and exhibitions (Mice) industries, with widespread cancellations of hotel bookings and seminars.

items.title: Civil groups challenge ban on recruits living with HIV
items.desc: Civil society groups are urging the Royal Thai Police to repeal a regulation barring people living with HIV from joining the force.

items.title: Price stability efforts keep ramping up
items.desc: The Ministry of Commerce has unveiled a fresh set of measures to ease the cost of living, increasing the number of controlled goods to 71 and requiring prior approval for price increases on 21 products.

items.title: Fuel prices at petrol stations soar by six baht a litre Thursday morning
items.desc: Retail petrol and diesel prices soared by six baht per litre on Thursday morning due to subsidy reduction by the state Oil Fuel Fund Executive Committee on Wednesday.

items.title: Akara vows to appeal court ruling
items.desc: Akara Resources Plc says it will appeal a Civil Court ruling ordering the company to compensate villagers for alleged environmental and health impacts linked to its gold mining operations, while reaffirming its compliance with regulatory standards.

items.title: Agencies spend B250m on trips
items.desc: The Anti-Corruption Organisation of Thailand has urged state agencies to stop overseas study trips that are largely tourism-oriented.

items.title: 19 state projects set for special funding
items.desc: Nineteen projects from five state agencies have secured funding under the Mekong-Lancang Special Fund (MLCSF) for public health, the green economy and agricultural modernisation.

items.title: Korn slams fuel crisis policies
items.desc: Democrat list-MP Korn Chatikavanij on Wednesday criticised the government's handling of the fuel supply crisis with shortages in several provinces, saying it has failed to act against alleged hoarding networks.

items.title: CPN unveils B110bn investment strategy
items.desc: SET-listed Central Pattana (CPN) has announced its five-year investment plan worth 110 billion baht covering 2026 to 2030.

items.title: Amity targets IPO after $100m AI funding round
items.desc: Amity, a Thailand-founded artificial intelligence (AI) company, has marked Southeast Asia's largest generative AI funding round through the successful completion of a US$100-million Series D fundraising.

items.title: Fuel subsidies threaten Thai credit rating
items.desc: Thailand is at risk of a credit downgrade if the Middle East war drags on for three months, resulting in fuel subsidies amounting to 200 billion baht, says Tisco's Economic Strategy Unit (ESU).

items.title: SEC escalates case against Bitazza, FreedomVerse
items.desc: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has filed a criminal complaint against Bitazza and FreedomVerse, along with three senior executives, for allegedly operating a digital asset dealer business without proper authorisation, intensifying regulatory scrutiny in the country's fast-evolving crypto sector.

items.title: Siren's Kiss keeps viewers guessing
items.desc: Adapted from a Japanese romantic thriller, Koori Bo Sekai (Ice World), Siren's Kiss is the South Korean version, starring renowned actors Park Min-young and Wi Ha-joon.

items.title: Book bargains galore at Bangkok fair
items.desc: More than 10 millions books are presented at special prices during the "54th National Book Fair & Bangkok International Book Fair 2026", which kicks off today and runs daily from 10am to 9pm until April 6, at Queen Sirikit National Convention Center's Hall 5-8, Ratchadaphisek Road.

items.title: Taste Australia's finest flavours at Tops stores nationwide
items.desc: Those who love Australian flavours have until March 31 to browse through more than 1,000 Australian products during "Discover Australia: Six States of Excellence", which is running at participating Tops and Tops Food Hall stores, as well as on Tops Online.

items.title: Iran rejects US peace proposal
items.desc: Iran has rejected a peace plan proposed by US President Donald Trump to end the Middle East war, state television reported on Wednesday.

items.title: The Chairman in Hong Kong is Asia’s best restaurant 2026
items.desc: HONG KONG — Cantonese cuisine trailblazer The Chairman in Hong Kong clinches the coveted title of The Best Restaurant in Asia, at the awards ceremony for Asia’s 50 Best Restaurants 2026 in Hong Kong, this evening.

items.title: Oil trading spike minutes before post by Trump raises questions
items.desc: LONDON - Traders bet half a billion dollars on the ‌price of crude only 15 minutes before US President Donald Trump announced a five-day delay to attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure that sent the market plunging, exchange data and Reuters calculations show.

items.title: 68 Indians held in Pattaya online gambling raids
items.desc: Police in Pattaya have dismantled two online gambling networks in a single day with a combined annual turnover of 2.3 billion baht, following separate raids on two villas that netted 68 Indian nationals.

items.title: Ang Thong oil depot probe making progress
items.desc: Police are stepping up an investigation into an oil depot in Ang Thong province after two partner companies submitted key documents, while authorities are also considering whether the case may involve fuel hoarding or sales at prices above controlled levels.

items.title: Top-tier teppanyaki
items.desc: Located on the 19th floor of Aman Nai Lert Bangkok as one of two Japanese dining concepts within the Aman Club, Hiori presents an elevated and intimate teppanyaki experience paired with attentive (nigh doting) service.

items.title: New releases for your streaming pleasure: Mar 25-31
items.desc: Looking for a title to binge-watch this weekend? Here's our pick!

items.title: Signs of jet fuel hoarding emerge in Asia
items.desc: Signs are growing that Asian countries are hoarding jet fuel after the Iran war sent oil prices surging, reflecting growing strain on the aviation industry.

items.title: Playfulness meets wellness
items.desc: Since opening its doors in December 2021 and being acquired by Hyatt in 2024, The Standard, Hua Hin, is doing some freshening up with an emphasis on wellness that combines fun and self-care.

items.title: Nothing brings Phone (4a) and Headphone (a) to Thailand
items.desc: London-based tech brand Nothing has officially landed in Thailand with the launch of the Phone (4a) Pro, Phone (4a) and its stylish over‑ear headphones, the Headphone (a), promising premium features at prices that do not require a deep breath or a long chat with your loved ones.

items.title: Roblox to introduce new controls to comply with child social media block
items.desc: JAKARTA — Videogame ⁠platform Roblox will introduce ​content and communications controls for players under 16 in ​Indonesia to comply

items.title: BTS concert drew 18.4 million viewers, says Netflix
items.desc: SEOUL — The comeback concert by Korean popular (K-pop) megastars BTS drew an estimated 18.4 million viewers worldwide, streaming giant Netflix said Wednesday.

items.title: Why the Japanese people love Thais so much, though there are still a few ‘what on earth?’ moments
items.desc: Why the Japanese people love Thais so much, though there are still a few ‘what on earth?’ moments Legacy According to a regular annual survey by the Japanese Cabinet Office, Thailand consistently ranks among the countries Japanese people feel most warmly towards, usually second only to Taiwan. Japanese tourists in Thailand often seem especially relaxed. They appear more cheerful, open and at ease than they do back home. They smile more easily, speak to Thai people more freely, and some return year after year. Some even decide to settle here for good. Thailand has the second-largest

items.title: Thai snake rescuer shares safety warning after cobra escapes inside car
items.desc: Thai snake rescuer shares safety warning after cobra escapes inside car Legacy A Thai snake rescuer and YouTuber shared a video of a cobra emerging inside a moving car, saying he and a friend avoided injury after the snake escaped from a fertiliser bag during transport. Nirut “Nick” Chom-ngam, better known online as Nick Wildlife , posted the clip on Facebook on March 23 and wrote in the caption, “A snake out in the car. We don’t know what to do at the moment. This is a true story, not a staged one for engagement“. In the video, the snake, reported to be a cobra, is seen moving across the stee

items.title: Actor files complaint after AI porn clip posing as him is sold online
items.desc: Actor files complaint after AI porn clip posing as him is sold online Legacy A Thai film actor, along with a well-known director, filed a police complaint today, March 25, after the actor’s face and personal information were used in a fake pornographic clip scam that was promoted and sold across social media. The complaint was lodged at the Central Investigation Bureau’s reporting centre by 25 year old actor Khunnapat Pichetworawut , accompanied by film director Phontharet Chotikridsadasophon. They asked investigators to take legal action against those responsible for editing and distributing

items.title: Thai woman accuses Mukdahan monk of abortion pressure
items.desc: Thai woman accuses Mukdahan monk of abortion pressure Legacy A Thai woman accused a monk in Mukdahan province, with whom she has a relationship , of being unfaithful and pressuring her to have an abortion . The woman posted on Facebook in recent days to express her anger against the monk. She shared photos showing them holding hands and kissing on the forehead, and also included an ultrasound image taken at a hospital during her pregnancy. In the post, she said the monk had left a “stigma” on her life and claimed her situation worsened despite the monk knowing she had faced difficulties. After

items.title: Myanmar woman arrested over alleged illegal migrant recruitment
items.desc: Myanmar woman arrested over alleged illegal migrant recruitment Legacy A Myanmar woman wanted under a court warrant was arrested in Samut Sakhon today, March 25, as immigration police reported progress in a case involving the alleged illegal recruitment of migrant workers . Police Colonel Sumet Jenwongphithak, chief of Samut Sakhon Immigration, said officers arrested 38 year old Khin during an operation targeting an alleged illegal migrant labour network in the province. Khin was wanted under an arrest warrant issued by the Samut Sakhon Provincial Court. Investigators said they tracked her dow

items.title: 泰移民局边境拘捕老挝籍嫌疑人 涉在曼谷杀害女友
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 泰国移民局廊开府办公室通报称，3月24日17时左右，廊开府移民局接到协调请求，要求协助拦截一名24岁的老挝籍嫌疑人Tom。该嫌疑人系泰国刑事法院于3月24日签发的通缉令所列对象，涉嫌故意杀人等罪名，并与一名老挝籍女子死亡案件有关。 通报称，死者20岁的为老挝籍女性Ram，居住在曼谷Lak Si区Chaeng Watthana14巷一处公寓。嫌疑人Tom随后向廊开府边境方向逃离，企图通过自然通道偷渡进入老挝。 廊开府移民局表示，执法人员在廊开府边境检查站区域蹲守并将Tom拘捕。执法人员表明身份后，核查其证件与护照信息，确认其为通缉令所列同一人，并依法告知其权利，随后将其带回办理相关手续。 经讯问，Tom供述其于2025年8月19日从廊开府口岸入境泰国，并在曼谷Lak Si区Thung Song Hong分区一所医院从事厨师工作，居住在Lak Si区Chaeng Watthana14巷公寓。其自称与老挝籍女友Ram交往，Ram在春武里府一处酒吧担任服务员。 Tom称，其曾要求女友停止相关工作，但对方拒绝并提出分手，理由包括其脾气暴躁及存在肢体暴力行为。他因嫉妒而追随女友并将其带回同住。3月22日，双方发生激烈争吵，其称在情绪失控情况下导致女友死亡。为掩盖事实，他在居所内使用厨房短刀对遗体进行肢解，并将遗体分装在其于便利店购买的垃圾袋内，共计8袋。其后，他通过应用程序呼

items.title: 尚泰公司未来拟投资超1100亿泰铢扩张 重点布局主要城市与旅游
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月25日，泰国最大购物中心开发商尚泰商业发展公共有限公司表示，计划在未来5年投资超过1100亿泰铢（约34亿美元），以扩展其项目组合，并押注主要城市增长与旅游复苏。 该公司运营曼谷大型购物中心综合体Central World。公司所属家族构建了泰国大型零售网络，覆盖购物中心、百货商店及综合用途开发项目。其母公司尚泰集团亦通过收购扩展海外业务，涉及欧洲高端资产，包括意大利的La Rinascente及伦敦的Selfridges，并持有德国KaDeWe和瑞士Globus的股权。 公司目前运营45个购物中心、11栋写字楼、17家酒店及53个住宅项目。公司去年净利润同比增长13%，达到创纪录的188亿泰铢；公司股价今年上涨约15%，与泰国SET指数的涨幅大体一致。 公司首席执行官瓦拉雅于周三向媒体表示，计划将综合用途项目数量在2030年底前由目前的27个增至33个。投资将用于新建零售商业项目、写字楼及一体化开发项目，重点聚焦主要城市与关键旅游目的地，包括曼谷、普吉和清迈。 瓦拉雅表示，尽管地缘政治局势紧张可能影响出行需求，公司仍对泰国长期发展潜力保持高度乐观，并称泰国曾多次度过困难时期，此次扩张体现了公司对主要城市与旅游枢纽消费韧性的信心。泰国经济对旅游与国内消费依赖度较高，游客流量变化可能迅速影响客流与零售销售，而出口疲弱也会削弱经济增长缓冲。 瓦拉雅还表

items.title: 25 Indians arrested for turning Pattaya villa into online gambling base
items.desc: 25 Indians arrested for turning Pattaya villa into online gambling base Legacy Police arrested 25 Indian nationals after conducting a raid on a villa in Pattaya that was converted into an operational base for three online gambling websites, with more than 700 million baht circulated through the platforms. Officers from Immigration Division 3 raided the villa in Soi Nong Mai Kaen 19 yesterday, March 24, following a tip-off that the property was being used to run illegal online gambling. Police said the villa had tight security, with the front gate covered by thick sunshades to block outsiders f

items.title: How Thailand’s construction industry is turning to ERP systems for control, transparency, and scale
items.desc: How Thailand’s construction industry is turning to ERP systems for control, transparency, and scale Legacy Thailand’s construction industry is entering a decisive phase of transformation. With large-scale infrastructure projects accelerating across the country, particularly under initiatives such as the Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC), contractors are facing increasing pressure to deliver projects faster, more efficiently, and with tighter cost control. This shift is not only about scale. It is about complexity. Modern construction projects involve multiple stakeholders, international supply c

items.title: Mother questions law after dog bite leaves no one accountable
items.desc: Mother questions law after dog bite leaves no one accountable Legacy A mother in Pak Kret district, Nonthaburi, is asking agencies to intervene and questioning the law after her 12 year old son was bitten by a dog near their housing complex on March 23, and no one has taken responsibility for it . A reporter visited the area yesterday, March 24, after 43 year old Saranya raised the complaint with the media. Saranya said her son was hurt when a dog chased and bit him, and she later reported the case to Pak Kret Police Station. Police reportedly told her it was unclear who could be charged becau

items.title: 泰国总理阿努廷乘比亚迪赴总理府办公
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月25日，据泰媒报道，泰国总理阿努廷当天前往总理府执行公务时，乘坐其新近购入的比亚迪（BYD）纯电动车出行，以实际行动响应政府节能政策，并应对当前能源危机带来的压力。 据悉，阿努廷当天乘坐的是一辆深灰色比亚迪海狮7（BYD SEALION 7），悬挂曼谷红牌车牌“ฎ 9798”。消息称，这辆新车是阿努廷在近一两天内刚刚购入的。 当天上午9时20分，阿努廷乘坐该车前往国会大厦，出席众议院会议。会议议程之一，是审议有关应对中东地区冲突引发能源危机的紧急动议。 随后，中午12时50分，阿努廷继续乘坐该车前往总理府办公。值得注意的是，阿努廷当天坐在副驾驶位置，且未安排开道车及随行车队，整体出行安排较以往明显简化。 报道称，此举正值国际油价波动、泰国面临能源压力之际，也与政府近期持续推动的节能减耗措施形成呼应。 此外，有消息称，3月25日下午14时，阿努廷还将与财政部、商业部、能源部以及国家经济和社会发展委员会举行会议，就能源问题带来的相关影响展开讨论。在此之前，当天下午16时30分，泰国政府还将召开能源形势专题会议。该会议将由副总理兼交通部长皮帕主持，其同时担任中东地区冲突局势管理与跟踪中心主任。 （编译：WAN；来源：thairath） The post 泰国总理阿努廷乘比亚迪赴总理府办公 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

items.title: Pickup driver and companion flee with 100 litres of diesel
items.desc: Pickup driver and companion flee with 100 litres of diesel Legacy A pickup truck driver and a companion stole 100 litres of diesel worth 3,081 baht from a PTT petrol station in Hat Yai district, Songkhla province, with the station owner urging the suspects to return and pay before a police complaint is filed. The owner of the PTT station, Jiraporn Khunnam, posted CCTV footage of the incident on her Facebook page yesterday, March 24. She said the incident occurred on March 20 and claimed the suspects refuelled and drove off without paying . In the caption, she wrote… “Refuel for 3,081 bah

items.title: 警方突袭芭提雅某豪华别墅！25名印度人操控赌博网站，年流水达7亿！
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，泰国移民局第3区调查组展开突击行动，在春武里府邦拉蒙县农普鲁地区一处别墅内逮捕25名印度籍嫌疑人。执法人员发现，该别墅被用作大型网络赌博网站的运营据点，资金流转规模高达每年约7亿泰铢。 据悉，警方通过线人获取情报称，辖区内一豪华别墅被用于赌博网站运营窝点，移民局局长随即下令调查小组前往调查。 现场检查发现，别墅安保严密，车库被改造成封闭式玻璃办公室，门口及围栏使用帆布遮挡，以避免外界察觉内部活动。执法人员进入屋内时，发现共有25名印度籍人员分工工作，分别负责市场推广、财务管理及客户后台操作。 警方还发现，屋内配备发电机并储存大量冷冻食品，员工被要求长期留在屋内活动，以减少外出引起注意，显示出该团伙是有组织、有计划地从事非法网络赌博业务。 执法人员控制现场后，对电脑设备等进行了检查，发现该团伙与3个大型赌博网站有关，网站名称均以“777”结尾。系统记录显示，资金流动平均每天约560万卢比，折算为泰铢约每年7亿泰铢。 随后，警方当场查扣笔记本电脑、手机等涉案设备共80余件。经核查，所有嫌疑人均持旅游签证入境泰国，涉嫌“外国人无工作许可从事工作或从事超出许可范围的工作”。 目前，全部嫌疑人已被移交农普鲁警局依法处理。警方表示，将继续扩大调查，追查幕后资金来源及相关网络，涉及人员可能包括泰国人及外国人。 （编译：jinda；审校：wan） The post 警

items.title: Czech influencer and MMA fighter killed in Koh Samui crash
items.desc: Czech influencer and MMA fighter killed in Koh Samui crash Legacy A Czech influencer and MMA fighter died on March 17 after her scooter was hit by a lorry on Koh Samui , Surat Thani, while she was on her way to a Muay Thai training session in Thailand. The woman was identified as 23 year old Dominika Elischerova, who was known online as Mina. She was taken to hospital with serious injuries following the collision. Photo via Instagram: @minaisbest A teammate, Samir Margina, said he witnessed the crash and later spoke about her condition. He told CNN Prima that her heart had stopped several time

items.title: “抱莲花拍照”成泰国Z世代OOTD打卡新趋势
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月24日，曼谷Phra Nakhon地区的老牌花市Pak Khlong花市呈现出全新的热闹景象，Z世代年轻人纷纷前来选购鲜花，用作拍照道具或时尚搭配。其中，近期最受欢迎的单品是莲花。 过去，以买花拍照为主的潮流，多偏向清新韩系风格，花卉选择多为郁金香、向日葵或雏菊等，而此次选择莲花的风潮，则清晰反映出Z世代更深层的审美取向与思考方式，他们不仅关注外在美感，也重视花朵所承载的象征意义与文化内涵。莲花所传达的宁静与纯净之美，成为这一趋势火遍全网的重要原因。 这一现象也展现了新一代年轻人的创造力。他们将与泰国文化及佛教密切相关的花卉重新诠释，使其转化为能够体现个性与风格的时尚元素。与莲花合影的画面呈现出柔和、沉静且富有艺术层次的氛围，与其他花卉拍照风格形成明显差异。 当选好心仪的花束后，年轻人通常会前往不远处的拉玛一世纪念桥拍照打卡。桥后方是湄南河，能够拍出自然又具有生活感的照片，其整体风格偏向轻松随性的氛围。 对于想要在这里出片的人，可以参考以下建议： 1.桥上风力较大，长发者应提前整理发型或准备发饰，以防头发凌乱影响拍摄效果。 2.建议提前想好想要的花种，避免在市场中花费过多时间寻找。除了莲花之外，郁金香、牡丹、雏菊、满天星、向日葵或兔尾草等，也都是不错的拍照选择。 3.若在傍晚拍摄，可获得温暖柔和的金色光线；若在夜间拍摄，建议开启闪光灯，使人物更突出

items.title: 一泰船已安全通过霍尔木兹海峡
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月24日，伊朗驻曼谷使馆发布消息称，已允许一泰国船只安全通过霍尔木兹海峡，使馆表示，谨向泰国人民通报，得益于两国以及阿曼苏丹国之间的密切合作，一艘泰国船只已平稳通过霍尔木兹海峡。伊方珍视友谊，不会忘记朋友。 泰国外交部长西哈萨表示，上述获准通行的船只隶属Bangchak公司，另有一艘仍滞留在相关海域的泰国船只，船东为暹罗水泥化学公司。 西哈萨还表示，他与伊朗外长阿巴斯通电话讨论了2项议题，其中之一是协助处置泰国货船Mayuree Naree号。该船于2026年3月11日遭袭后漂移进入伊朗水域。此前阿曼方面已协助救起20名船员，另有3名船员去向不明。伊朗驻泰大使当日致电泰国外交部门通报称，伊朗与阿曼已开展联合行动，目前已获得进入Mayuree Naree号的通道，并已接触到3名船员。但泰方表示，仍在核实3人是否仍然生还。 另外，他向伊朗方面提出的第二项议题，是希望伊朗确保泰国船只在霍尔木兹海峡的安全通行。他表示，泰国并非相关冲突当事方，霍尔木兹海峡船舶安全航行应适用国际法规则。泰方并称，已获悉Bangchak公司所属船只于昨日安全通过霍尔木兹海峡，目前正驶往泰国，并希望另一艘泰国船只也能安全返航。 （编译：hansa ；审核：wan；来源：khaosod） The post 一泰船已安全通过霍尔木兹海峡 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻

items.title: 泰国2月汽车产量同比增长3.43%
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日，泰国工业联合会（FTI）汽车工业集团公布了今年2月以及1-2月的汽车生产、出口和内销数据。就2月单月的数据来看，汽车产量同比增长3.43%、出口同比微降0.05%、内销同比减少2.17%。当前仍须密切注意出口市场，因为中东战争可能对汽车的出口运输造成影响。 2月全国共生产各类汽车117,952辆，较去年同期增长3.43%。其中，出口型乘用车产量增加22.83%、内销型皮卡产量增加55.98%。1-2月累计生产汽车236,338辆，同比增长6.87%。 2月全国总出口汽车81,195辆，较1月增长39.02%，但较去年同期减少41辆，增长率微降0.05%。1-2月累计出口完整汽车139,600辆，较去年同期减少2.76%。 汽车工业集团顾问兼发言人素拉蓬表示，出口减少的市场包括亚洲、澳洲与大洋洲、欧洲。中东市场因1-2月尚未发生实际交战仍保持增长，但目前向中东运输车辆的船只在抵达霍尔木兹海峡后不敢继续航行，只能暂时停靠在印度和新加坡等待。 中东是泰国汽车出口的第三大市场，在当地，汽车几乎所有国家向其出口价值排名第一的商品，所以必须密切关注战事何时结束。2025年，泰国向中东出口汽车200,001辆，较2024年增长0.61%，占全年出口总量（935,750辆）的21.17%，价值超过1,200亿泰铢，其中以皮卡最多，为114,644辆；乘用车61,958

items.title: “阿努廷2.0”内阁名单已送交资格审查
items.desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月24日16点40分，泰国总理兼内政部部长、泰自豪党党魁阿努廷在参加党内会议后透露，他已叮嘱所有议员重视国会的工作。在3月25日的国会会议上，泰自豪党将针对能源议题提出动议，他本人则没有需要特别说明的事项，但新政府愿意听取各方议员的建议、意见和批评，只要是能让事情往更好方向发展的，都应该认真听取。 当被问到是否有需要紧急推动的法律时，阿努廷回答说，有多部在上届积压的法律需要重新整理或修改后再次提出。 关于新内阁的组建时间表，阿努廷表示，目前各部长人选的名单已经送去资格审查，以确认是否符合宪法规定，或是否有任何人选与宪法相抵触。根据他从内阁秘书处了解到的信息，名单已送交18个独立机构审查，因为每个机构都是独立运作的，所以无法规定必须在几天内答复。人选资格审查完成后会送回内阁秘书处，再转交国务委员会秘书处，预计大约需要一周的时间。不过，阿努廷不愿告知新部长人选的总人数是否为35人，他只说，他已经把名单提交了。 至于国务委员会秘书长帕空将出任副总理这一传闻，阿努廷没有正面回答，而是反问说，有谁看到部长人选名单了吗，通常在国王御准之前，部长的任命事宜都不宜提前公开，所以这个问题他无法回答。 另外，阿努廷说，如果有人选的资格出现问题，就会更换成其他人，但流程也会拉长，因为内阁秘书处已告知，在这种情况下可能需要额外增加5天时间。泰自豪党的备用名单已自行做好安排，其他政党的事
